summary: First real-weight e2e of Whisper word timestamps (M4-20a) ran on base+turbo x jfk+2 LibriSpeech via a scratch harness (surface is library-only: transcribe_tokens_beam_nbest + BeamSearchConfig.word_timestamps; no CLI flag, no HTTP exposure); cached GGUFs carry vokra.whisper.alignment_heads whose per-model sets EXACTLY match openai-whisper (base 8 heads, turbo 6). The pipeline works end to end — words deterministic, monotone, correct sequence in 5/6 combos — but raw per-word deltas vs openai-whisper 20250625 are mean 212-443 ms / p95 440-1280 ms, and the cause is a P1 off-by-one: beam_glue.rs whisper_word_timings slices at text_lo = n_prefix (4, decoder_start_ids INCLUDES no_timestamps) while openai timing.py:215 slices at len(sot_sequence) (3, EXCLUDES it), dropping the first-token emission row so every vokra word start lands at openai's word END; the diagnostic |v.start(k)-r.end(k)| collapses to mean 30-69 ms / p95 40-220 ms across all 6 combos, proving the DTW core itself matches within tens of ms. Second consequence: every clip's final word ends at 30.000 s (final_time = padded-window n_ctx*dt instead of openai's eot-emission arrival), failing the [0, audio_len] sanity on all 6 combos; also the caller feeds the full padded 1500-frame window instead of the num_frames//2 valid-frame restriction the CrossAttention docstring requires. Speed: the alignment pass itself is cheap (+2.1-3.2% on base; turbo interleaved paired median +5.8% — session-1's +79.8% was contention artifact at load avg 28, superseded), but reaching word timestamps requires the beam surface whose per-step full-prefix recompute costs +9.7%/+50.6%/+93.1% vs incremental greedy on base. jfk first-word sanity passes against the true onset 0.322 s — the assignment's "~5.15 s" is sample index 5152, an errata. All raw outputs, per-word TSVs, and scripts under /Users/inamotoyuuta/.cache/vokra-eval/out/wt-whisper/.

accuracy.reference: openai-whisper 20250625 (semantics SoT), word_timestamps=True, greedy temp=0, language=en, fp16=False, condition_on_previous_text=False; models base + large-v3-turbo (MIT); matching via difflib on normalized words (lowercase, alnum+apostrophe; vokra standalone punctuation folded into neighbor = labeled merge_punctuations emulation)
accuracy.alignment_heads_metadata: GGUF vs openai model buffers EXACT match: base [(3,1),(4,2),(4,3),(4,7),(5,1),(5,2),(5,4),(5,6)] (8), turbo [(2,4),(2,11),(3,3),(3,6),(3,11),(3,14)] (6); small/medium GGUFs carry the key (values not dumped)
accuracy.word_sequence_identity_normalized: 5/6 combos identical; base/l0002 ratio 0.985 (vokra has one extra 'is' — decode-context difference: vokra decodes with notimestamps, openai with timestamp tokens)

raw_word_deltas_ms_mean_p95_max.note: start deltas, punct-merged, excl final-word end; final-word end delta reaches 19640 ms (30 s pad leak)

off_by_one_diagnostic_abs_vstart_minus_r.interpretation: collapse from hundreds of ms to tens of ms confirms one-emission-row shift; DTW core matches openai within tens of ms

accuracy.sanity: monotone starts 6/6 PASS; start<=end 6/6 PASS; within [0,audio_len] 0/6 FAIL (final '.' end=30.000 s on every clip + interior 65 ms overshoot base/l0000 'gospel' 5.920>5.855); jfk first word 0.0 s vs VAD onset anchor 0.322 s (=sample 5152) PASS; outputs bit-deterministic across 4 independent turbo runs

speed.window: per-iteration = transcribe call only (log-mel + encoder + full decode; beam-words adds one full-prefix decoder forward with cross-attention capture + DTW + word merge); excludes GGUF load, WAV read, detokenization; 1 warmup + 3 measured, median; RTF = median wall / TRUE clip secs (audio internally zero-padded to 30 s window)
speed.machine_caveat: M1 shared with sibling agents; 1-min load avg 13-28 during turbo session-1; base runs CV ~1% (reliable)
speed.overhead_alignment_words_vs_beamplain: base +3.2%/+2.1%/+2.1% (jfk/l0000/l0002); turbo interleaved paired deltas +1.36/+2.56/+2.87 s, median +2.56 s = +5.8% of median plain 43.86 s
speed.overhead_beam_surface_vs_incremental_gre: base +9.7%/+50.6%/+93.1% — per-step full-prefix recompute (O(T^2)); word timestamps are ONLY reachable via this beam surface
speed.turbo_session1_noise: beam-words medians 86.4 s vs beam-plain 48.1 s (+79.8%) was contention artifact — superseded by interleaved re-run; turbo greedy median 44.16 s (RTF 4.02)
speed.openai_context_not_like_for_like: base jfk words 0.83 s vs plain 0.56 s median (+48%), different load window

findings: P1: off-by-one in Whisper word-timestamp row slice — crates/vokra-models/src/whisper/beam_glue.rs whisper_word_timings uses text_lo = n_prefix (len(decoder_start_ids)=4, INCLUDING <|notimestamps|>) but openai timing.py:215 slices matrix[len(sot_sequence):-1] (3 tokens, EXCLUDING no_timestamps), so the emission row of the FIRST text token is dropped and every word start/end is reported one emission row late (~one word, mean 212-443 ms); shifted diagnostic collapses to 30-69 ms mean. Fix: text_lo = n_prefix-1, treat the n+1 rows as emitting [c_0..c_{n-1}, eot], use eot-row arrival as final word end

P2: final-word end = padded-window end — words_from_alignment final_time = n_ctx*dt = 30.0 s, so the last word ends at 30.000 s on every clip < 30 s (violates [0, audio_len] 6/6; openai bounds by eot-emission arrival; fixed by the same change as the P1)

P2: Whisper caller ignores the valid-frames contract — CrossAttention docstring requires weights restricted to num_frames//2 (openai weights[:,:,:num_frames//2]) but beam_glue feeds the full padded 1500-frame window; interior effect measured small on these clips but it stretches tails past true audio (base/l0000 'gospel' end 5.920 > 5.855 s)

P2 (perf): word timestamps are only reachable through transcribe_tokens_beam_nbest whose per-step full-prefix recompute (M0 recompute posture, beam_glue.rs WhisperLogitsSource) costs +9.7% to +93.1% vs incremental greedy on base — the alignment pass itself is only +2-6%; a greedy-path word-timestamp surface or per-beam KV cache would remove most of the cost

P3: no merge_punctuations equivalent — vokra emits standalone punctuation words ('.', ',') where openai folds them into neighbors (prepend/append_punctuations); consumers see 25 vokra words vs 22 openai words on jfk

P3: surface gaps — no vokra-cli flag for word timestamps and the HTTP layer (/v1/audio/transcriptions) has no timestamp_granularities; the field exists only on the internal TranscribeBeamRequest dispatch (HTTP beam endpoint not landed per service.rs doc)
P3 (campaign errata): assignment's 'jfk speech onset ~5.15 s' is campaign-1 VAD sample index 5152 = 0.322 s at 16 kHz (file is 11.0 s); RMS confirms onset ~0.30 s

note: base/l0002 vokra transcript has one extra 'is' vs openai-whisper (decode-context difference — vokra decodes with <|notimestamps|>, openai transcribe() decodes with timestamp tokens; campaign-1 vokra-vs-ORT was byte-identical)
issues: turbo timing session-1 contention-noised (shared M1, load avg 13-28): beam-words iters [59.8, 86.4, 87.0] s produced a bogus +79.8% overhead — superseded by 3 interleaved plain/words pairs (median paired delta +2.56 s = +5.8%); raw values of both sessions preserved in vokra-matrix.log / vokra-turbo-interleave.log
openai-whisper reference wall times are context-only (different load window than vokra runs), cross-engine speed is campaign-1 scope
turbo LibriSpeech words runs are 1-iteration (words extraction only, deterministic — verified bit-identical 4x on jfk; no timing claim)

wt-harness run whisper-turbo.gguf jfk-30s.wav {beam-plain,beam-words} 0 1 ...  # 3 interleaved pairs

summary: First real-weight vokra-server integration run (whisper-base.gguf + piper-plus-css10-ja-6lang-neutralspk.gguf, campaign-1 conversions, repo READ-ONLY @92dbc92): production startup wiring works — eager load to /health-ready median 0.638s, hard-error UX on missing GGUF and half-wired config (exit 1), graceful SIGTERM drain, zero panics across all probes. OpenAI POST /v1/audio/transcriptions returns the canonical JFK transcript byte-identically (11.0s audio, median 1.596s quiet-window loopback = RTF 0.145) and correct LibriSpeech text; all shape probes correct (text/plain 200, unknown model 404, verbose_json 501, typo multipart field 400, bad WAV 400); vLLM routes honor the contract-only 501/400 design; 4 parallel transcriptions all 200 with byte-identical bodies (no interleaving corruption). Wyoming works end-to-end ONLY with the repo's inline-data framing (canonical transcript; matched-load A/B shows HTTP and Wyoming routes equivalent; 5th concurrent connection gets explicit refusal at the 4-session cap, permits release cleanly). P1: with the genuine `wyoming` PyPI client framing (every version >=1.2.0, i.e. all Home-Assistant-era clients — pinned by pip-source bisect 1.0.0/1.2.0/1.4.0/1.5.2/1.10.0), describe/info discovery succeeds but ALL inference fails: pump_events (integrations/vokra-server/src/api/wyoming.rs:645-653) reads the data_length continuation then discards it, so audio-start/audio-chunk error "missing data" and synthesize loses its text — contradicting the repo's own wyoming-design.md §3 which passes data_buf to dispatch. P1: the production binary never injects the real G2P (InferenceService::build installs PassthroughPhonemizer; with_phonemizer unused by main path), so plain-text TTS fails with an explicit error on every surface, while raw phoneme-id input synthesizes correctly (1.196s non-silent 22050 Hz audio, deterministic 4/4, TTFA median 663ms ~= total because synth is batch). P2: the FR-SV-04 /api/tts router exists in code (piper_http.rs dispatch_tts) but is never mounted in build_http_app -> production HTTP TTS is 404.
findings: P1 (wyoming protocol compat): inbound event `data` continuation is read then DISCARDED by pump_events (integrations/vokra-server/src/api/wyoming.rs:645-653 — `data` buffer never merged into header.data before PumpItem::Event(header, payload)). Upstream wyoming >=1.2.0 (through current 1.10.0; HA-era clients) pops `data` out of the header and sends it ONLY in that continuation, so a real Home Assistant satellite gets: audio-start -> error 'audio-start/chunk missing data', audio-chunk -> same, transcribe -> 'no audio received before transcribe', synthesize -> text lost -> 'PassthroughPhonemizer: empty input'. Live-reproduced with the genuine wyoming 1.10.0 package (logs/wyoming-upstream-framing.json); only describe/info (empty data) interoperates, and only the client->server direction is broken (upstream readers do accept the server's inline-data replies). Contradicts the repo's own docs/wyoming-design.md §3 whose canonical loop passes data_buf to dispatch. Fix = merge data continuation into header.data (upstream read semantics), plus repo tests currently encode the wrong (inline-only, <=1.1.x) client framing.

P1 (production TTS unusable with text): the production startup path (server.rs build_service -> InferenceService::build) installs PassthroughPhonemizer (raw phoneme ids / [[symbol]] literals only) and never calls with_phonemizer to inject the real 8-language G2P (integrations/vokra-piper-g2p) — so every plain-text synthesize fails: 'PassthroughPhonemizer: `Hello` is not a phoneme id (use [[symbol]] for symbols)'. Wyoming TTS (and any future /api/tts) can only synthesize from pre-phonemized input; campaign-1's CLI --text placeholder-tokenizer gap applies server-side in stricter (hard-error) form. Raw phoneme-id input works e2e (info/audio-start/30 chunks/audio-stop, 26368 samples @22050 Hz = 1.196 s, peak |i16| 7419, deterministic).

P2 (FR-SV-04 route unmounted): api/piper_http.rs implements the /api/tts schema + dispatch_tts but no router mount exists; build_http_app (server.rs:228-240) merges only openai + vllm routers -> POST /api/tts = 404 on the production binary (logs/api-tts-probe.txt). The crate's own piper_http_compat.rs tolerates 404 as 'T11/T12 router not yet wired', so no test forces the mount.
P3 (vLLM/OpenAI discovery): GET /v1/models is not routed (404). OpenAI/vLLM SDK clients typically probe it before calling audio/completions endpoints.

P3 (async hygiene): the OpenAI transcriptions_handler runs the multi-second Whisper transcribe synchronously inside the async handler (openai.rs transcriptions_handler -> transcribe_to_response, no spawn_blocking), blocking a tokio worker per in-flight request; the Wyoming path correctly uses spawn_blocking. Survived the 4-way smoke (8 workers) but is an availability hazard at higher fan-in.

P3 (beam surface unreachable over HTTP): TranscribeBeamRequest/transcribe_beam (M3-15 Wave 10/11) is exposed only at trait level; no HTTP route accepts beam_size/n-best, so the feature is untestable end-to-end from a client.
issues: Shared-machine confound: sibling agents drove load avg from quiet to 18-26 mid-session; every timing row in results.tsv records its load context. Early HTTP medians (spread <1%) are from the quiet window; Wyoming absolute latencies were load-inflated, and an interleaved matched-load A/B (HTTP 3.770/3.970/3.859 s vs Wyoming 3.869/3.773/4.126 s) proves route parity — the initially-observed 2.15x Wyoming gap was a load artifact, not a route difference (logs/ab-http-vs-wyoming.txt).
corpus jfk-30s.wav actually contains 11.0 s of audio (176000 frames @16 kHz; name is historical) — RTFs computed on 11.0 s.
OpenAI `language`/`temperature`/`prompt` multipart fields are accepted and deliberately ignored at T06 (documented in-code as T07-followup) — not counted as a new finding.
TTS language-id not addressable through the server surface (SynthesisRequest::new(text) only), so the 6-lang voice ran with its default language embedding for the raw-id probe.
GGUFs reused from campaign 1 predate the 4 P1 fixes, but those fixes touch silero/cosyvoice2/voxtral/kokoro converters, not whisper-base/piper — reconversion not required for this leg.
Not exercised (scope): whisper-large-v3/kokoro/voxtral/silero server slots, TOML + env config layers, mid-TTS barge-in race, musl static build (FR-SV-01).

summary: Closed the T29 blocker and made the real-weight Mimi PCM roundtrip run end-to-end: the converter now emits the vokra.mimi.* config chunk group (shape-derived + loaders.py constants) and 284 structural mimi.enc.*/mimi.dec.* tensors (transformer linears transposed to w_t, fused in_proj split, channel-wise upsample zero-expanded to dense — the checkpoint has plain fused weights, so no weight_norm fusion was ever needed), while the runtime gained two upstream-fidelity fixes discovered by reading moshi 0.2.13 source: replicate left-pad on the frame-resample downsample and the SplitResidualVectorQuantizer parallel-projection encode (semantic 1-deep + acoustic 31-deep chains, presence-driven via mimi.enc.input_proj_rest). Acceptance vs upstream moshi on jfk (10.96 s, 137 frames): code agreement 4384/4384 = 100.0% (independent engines, cross-decodes differ on 98% of samples at fp-noise), same-codes decode PCM max|Δ| 3.67e-6 / mel-L1 2.99e-5, roundtrip mel-L1 to input 0.3166817 both engines (equal to 7 s.f.). Speed (1+3 median, single-thread CPU, whole-buffer window, bind excluded): vokra encode RTF 0.503 / decode 0.378 vs upstream torch(1-thread) 0.043 / 0.040 — vokra 11.7x/9.5x slower, honest negative. DAC quantizer-stage parity re-verified on current HEAD with the new GGUF (106 PARITY rows PASS, 0 failed); DAC waveform SEANet remains an unimplemented known gap per assignment. Verify all green: vokra-models 700 lib + suites, vokra-convert 130 (12 new tests incl. env-gated VOKRA_MIMI_GGUF roundtrip), fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean; patch emitted, nothing committed.

accuracy.input: jfk-30s.wav resampled 16k->24k (torchaudio.functional.resample), trimmed to 137 whole frames = 263040 samples = 10.96 s; identical f32 buffer fed to both engines

code_agreement_vokra_vs_upstream.provenance_check: files written by independent engines (rust harness / python moshi); identical sha256 IS the result — cross-engine PCM decodes differ on 257791/263040 samples at fp-noise, proving distinct implementations

roundtrip_mel_l1_to_input.note: codec reconstruction loss dominates; engine delta is 4 orders below it

accuracy.gated_test: VOKRA_MIMI_GGUF roundtrip: 138 frames -> 4416 codes -> 264960 samples, rms 0.3087, PASS
accuracy.dac_reverify: campaign-1 real_codec_parity command on current HEAD + new mimi GGUF: 2 passed / 0 failed, 106 PARITY rows PASS (mimi decoded_features 1.526e-5, dac codebooks bit-exact); DAC waveform SEANet still unimplemented (known deferred WP)

speed.protocol: 1 warmup + 3 measured, median wall / 10.96 s audio; window = whole-buffer encode()/decode() with model pre-loaded/pre-bound (GGUF open+bind excluded, reported separately); fresh streaming state per call; single-thread CPU both sides (torch.set_num_threads(1))
speed.ratio: vokra 11.7x (encode) / 9.5x (decode) slower than 1-thread torch — honest negative, consistent with campaign-1 CPU-speed findings

findings: P1 (FIXED in patch): Mimi PCM roundtrip was unwired — converter emitted no vokra.mimi.* neural-chain config and no structural tensors; campaign-1 error reproduced verbatim, closed converter-side per the module-doc T29 design

P1 (FIXED in patch): runtime encoder RVQ semantics diverged from upstream — single input_proj + 32-deep chain vs upstream SplitResidualVectorQuantizer's TWO parallel projections (rvq_first 1-deep semantic + rvq_rest 31-deep acoustic, both over the same latent); would have produced wrong acoustic codes even with names bound; fixed presence-driven (mimi.enc.input_proj_rest), old single-chain behavior preserved when absent

P2 (FIXED in patch): frame-resample downsample uses pad_mode=replicate upstream (first-chunk left-context = its first column) but the runtime zero-padded everything — first-frame codes would differ; PadMode::Replicate transcribed from conv.py with streaming bit-exactness pinned

P2 (FIXED exactly, perf follow-up open): upsample conv-transpose is channel-wise [512,1,4] (upstream 'upsample_channel_wise_bug=True'); converter zero-expands to dense [512,512,4] — mathematically exact but 512x FLOPs on that stage; a runtime grouped-convtr would be the performant fix

P2 (OPEN): vokra Mimi CPU speed is 11.7x (encode) / 9.5x (decode) slower than single-thread torch on M1 — per-position transformer stepping, scalar im2col gathers, dense-expanded upsample are the visible contributors

P3 (OPEN): standalone Mimi GGUF grew 519MB -> 906MB because structural tensors are derived duplicates next to the ADR M4-04 §D-f raw pass-through; consider gating the pass-through of mapped tensors in a follow-up

P3 (corrected in docs): the module-doc/campaign-1 hypothesis that weight_norm (g,v) fusion is needed was wrong — the real checkpoint stores plain fused conv weights (loaders.py: weights 'pre-processed for inference', norm='none'); rustdoc honest-boundary sections updated
issues: torchaudio 2.9.1 in venv-rvq cannot torchaudio.load() without torchcodec — worked around with stdlib wave for WAV I/O (functional.resample unaffected); raw error tail in out/mimi-roundtrip if needed
worktree base was 328e61b, not 92dbc92 — detached to 92dbc92 per HARD RULE 3 before any work

summary: Implemented all 3 bounded CLI fixes on worktree base 92dbc92 (detached from stale 328e61b): run --backend cpu|metal|cuda|vulkan (shared bench parse_backend, honored by ASR+speaker, explicit backend-unavailable error when feature not compiled — verified rc=1 for cuda/vulkan on the metal-only build), convert help now lists all 11 ModelKind spellings in both vokra-cli and standalone vokra-convert, and a new run speaker task (campplus arch -> ModelTask::Speaker, kaldi_fbank+SpeakerEncoder+speaker_verify, 16 kHz gate, --compare cosine). Verify green: vokra-cli 74+4 and vokra-convert 10+4 release tests pass (incl. 13 new), fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean on default AND --features metal; patch (6 files, +485/-23) at the required path. V2 is a bullseye through the NEW CLI path: intra-speaker cosine 0.872242 (exact match to campaign 1), inter 0.282770/0.343237 (deltas 0/-3e-6 vs 5dp references), L2 norms match campaign-1 to 6dp, --backend metal reproduces 0.872242 identically. V1 transcripts: whisper-small run --backend metal output is byte-identical to CPU and to the campaign-1 reference, and turbo CPU matches campaign-1 (comma variant included); small bench RTF = CPU 1.346 vs Metal 0.8056 (iters 3/warmup 1, session-preloaded window) but the M1 was contended by 4 sibling agents (campaign-1 uncontended refs: 0.691 CPU / 0.728 Metal), so no cross-campaign speed claims. V3 proved the delegate path: vokra-cli convert --model mimi produced a GGUF byte-identical (cmp) to campaign-1's mimi.gguf; the only open item at forced report time is the still-running V1 turbo bench/Metal leg (driver continues writing v1-*-whisper-turbo-metal.* and V1 DONE into out/cli-enablers/v1-driver.log).

summary: License gate PASS: the DFN3 checkpoint ships inside the dual MIT/Apache-2.0 Rikorose/DeepFilterNet repo itself (models/DeepFilterNet3.zip, sha256 49c52edc..., README L323-330 + models-dir listing saved as evidence), so the weight half proceeded. Central result: the real checkpoint cannot be converted or hosted — 133 conv+BatchNorm+GroupedLinear+GRU tensors (2.17M params incl. trained erb_fb/erb_inv_fb filterbanks) vs vokra's 6-tensor 263k-param dense scaffold with 0 name overlap, and vokra-convert has no checkpoint parser at all — so numeric parity is architecturally impossible without the op-level implementation explicitly deferred to owner T17 (no re-architecture attempted per scope; no converter-level fix can close it, hence no patch). Verified green on the vokra side: T12 GGUF write/load/forward exercised for the first time at real DFN3 dims (1,054,272 B, seed-42 synthetic), STFT-to-iSTFT chain-shell identity 3.576e-7, deep_filter_net3() constants match the shipped config.ini, and vokra's erb-scale formula is the same Glasberg-Moore family as libDF within 0.3%. Quality on the deterministic 5.000 dB-SNR noisy jfk (48 kHz, seeded): upstream real DFN3 lifts SI-SNR 5.00 to 14.77 dB, while the shipped op with synthetic weights outputs -29.17 dB (34 dB below the noisy input) — an honest negative proving v1.0-rc's denoise is a topology shell, not a functional denoiser. Stage bisect (the deliverable): divergence starts at stage 1 STFT — libDF uses a Vorbis window sin(pi/2*sin^2) plus wnorm=2*hop/fft^2=1/960 applied in analysis only, vs vokra Hann/no-scale (measured global scale 0.001197, 9.9% shape residual after scaling) — then ERB band partition 93.14% bin-identical (missing min_nb_erb_freqs=2), missing stateful erb_norm/unit_norm stages (affine fit r=0.65/0.42), no complex-spectrum DF encoder branch, and no df_lookahead=2, each pinned to fetched libDF source lines. Speed: upstream enhance() RTF 0.0233 vs vokra forward 0.0262 (CPU, median of 3 after warmup) — comparable wall time despite 8x fewer params, so a faithful conv+GRU port will be slower still without optimization.

accuracy.si_snr_methodology: zero-mean scale-invariant SI-SNR over the full 11.0 s signal at 48 kHz; input = corpus jfk-30s.wav (actually 11.0 s) sinc-resampled 16k->48k + np.random.default_rng(20260717) white noise scaled to 5 dB SNR over the full signal (measured 5.000 dB, peak 0.961)

cross_engine_delta_upstream_vs_vokra.note: meaningless-by-construction (vokra weights are synthetic; no trained network exists that fits the op) — measured and labeled per assignment step 5a; mel = torchaudio MelSpectrogram 48k/n_fft1024/hop256/80mels, log(mel+1e-5)

stft.root_cause: libDF Vorbis window sin(pi/2*sin^2(pi*(n+0.5)/N)) + wnorm=2*hop/fft^2=1/960 'in analysis only' (libdf-lib.rs L127-133, L389-393) vs vokra Hann periodic + Normalization::Backward; 0.001197 = (1/960) x 1.149 window-shape factor

erb_band_partition.detail: vokra bands 0-4 get 1 bin each vs upstream min_nb_erb_freqs=2 (erb_fb cumulative-round algorithm, libdf-lib.rs L68-100); erb scale formulas match within 0.3% (9.294 vs 9.265 ln-coefficient)

erb_feature_global_affine_fit_vokra_to_u.detail: upstream = 10*log10(width-normalized band power of wnorm'd spectrum + 1e-10) then erb_norm exp-decay mean normalization alpha 0.99; vokra = ln(mean band power + 1e-10) with NO normalization stage

vokra_green.t12_gguf_at_real_dfn3_dims: PASS (1,054,272 B write -> parse -> from_gguf -> forward)

speed.protocol: 1 warmup + 3 measured runs, median, Apple M1 CPU

upstream_dfn3.window: df.enhance.enhance() call = libdf STFT analysis + erb/erb_norm/unit_norm features + torch model forward (8 threads) + libdf synthesis + delay trim; model load and file I/O excluded

vokra_denoise_op.window: DenoiseModel::forward() = STFT + ERB pooling + scaffold net + DF FIR + iSTFT, single-threaded; GGUF load and file I/O excluded

speed.caveat: NOT like-for-like network cost (263k-param synthetic dense vs 2.14M-param conv+GRU): comparable wall despite 8x fewer params means vokra's hot path is the 256x960 per-frame dense df_decoder; a faithful port will be slower without optimization

findings: P1 dfn3-arch-gap: the M4-20(c) denoise op cannot load or host the real DeepFilterNet3 checkpoint — 0/133 tensor mapping onto the 6-tensor dense scaffold, vokra-convert has no checkpoint parser (upstream ships PyTorch .ckpt in .zip; converter consumes only a pre-built DenoiseModel), and with the only loadable weights (synthetic) the op DEGRADES audio by 34 dB SI-SNR below the noisy input. v1.0-rc ships an untrained topology shell behind the 'DeepFilterNet (MIT) 統合' claim; T17 requires op-level conv+GRU+filterbank implementation, not a converter fix. Ground-truth tensor inventory now available (ckpt-tensors.tsv).

P2 dfn3-frontend-drift: six pinned frontend divergences that will break parity even after a future weight port — (a) Vorbis window + wnorm=1/960 analysis normalization vs Hann/backward (measured scale 0.0012, 9.9% shape residual); (b) ERB partition algorithm (93.14% bins same band; missing min_nb_erb_freqs=2); (c) 10*log10 vs ln band features; (d) missing stateful erb_norm (alpha 0.99) and unit_norm stages; (e) no complex-spectrum DF encoder branch (upstream feeds unit_norm(spec[..,:96]) to dedicated enc.df_conv*); (f) causal-only DF FIR vs df_lookahead=2 + trained mask.erb_inv_fb gain expansion. Each pinned to fetched libDF source lines (saved under license/).

P3 dfn3-cli-doc-drift: crates/vokra-ops/src/denoise.rs docstring references a 'vokra-cli convert --model denoise' entry (T12) that does not exist — grep of crates/vokra-cli/src has zero denoise hits; only library fns convert_denoise_synthetic/convert_denoise_from_model are exported.

summary: Un-defer decision material complete: the canonical UTMOS22-strong ckpt (epoch=3-step=7459.ckpt, 1,238,128,841 B, sha256 44c57e3e…) and its wav2vec2-base SSL (wav2vec_small.pt, 950,500,491 B, byte-count-identical to fairseq's canonical dl.fbaipublicfiles.com URL) are anonymously obtainable from the MIT-licensed HF space sarulab-speech/UTMOS-demo (rev 47212055). The license chain is fully permissive — space MIT + UTMOS22 GitHub MIT + fairseq MIT (HF wav2vec2-base apache-2.0) — and the BVCC fine-tune data (Zenodo 10691660, "other-open") carries only a Blizzard-sample redistribution ban with NO academic-only clause found anywhere, so the fail-closed trigger is not tripped (T02 sign-off remains owner-fired, with a training-data-provenance caveat to record). State-dict recon (225 tensors / 103,156,993 params) shows the real stack = a v0-expressible wav2vec2-base trunk (7-conv 512ch k[10,3,3,3,3,2,2] s[5,2,2,2,2,2,2], feat_proj 512→768, 12×768/12h/3072 post-norm blocks, mean-after pooling, score=mean·2+3) PLUS 8 v0-inexpressible parts (GroupNorm on conv0, feature LN[512], weight-norm-parameterized pos_conv k=128 g=16, encoder input LN[768], DomainEmbedding[3,128] const id 0, judge Embedding[3000,128] const id 288, BLSTM 1024→512×2, head ReLU) — the ADR-anticipated wav2vec2_regression.v1 bump is confirmed required and no phoneme/G2P path is needed (released ckpt has no PhonemeEncoder keys). A kernel gap was found: no LSTM or GroupNorm kernel exists in vokra-backend-cpu/vokra-ops and Kokoro's BiLstm1d sits behind the banned vokra-eval→vokra-models edge. DNSMOS live evidence contradicts the research-only fear (repo Legal Notices: content CC-BY-4.0 + code MIT, P.835 ONNX models committed in-repo, no research-only clause) so T03 is likely resolvable as GO-with-attribution; estimated flip scope ≈ 9-12 CC tickets (~4.5-6 h), full memo at decision-memo.md.

grep/py key-set diff wav2vec-small-state-dict.tsv vs utmos22-strong-state-dict.tsv (SSL portion): 218 vs 211 tensors, delta = quantizer.{vars,weight_proj.*} + project_q.* + final_proj.* (7 pretraining-only)
artifacts: /Users/inamotoyuuta/.cache/vokra-eval/out/utmos-probe/evidence/ (26 raw evidence files: HF/GitHub/Zenodo API JSON, LICENSE texts, space code, sha256, HTTP headers)
findings: P2: real UTMOS22-strong is NOT expressible in ARCH_VARIANT_V0 — flip requires the ADR-anticipated wav2vec2_regression.v1 with 8 concrete additions: GroupNorm affine[512] on conv layer 0 only, feature-extractor LayerNorm[512], weight-norm-parameterized positional conv (weight_g[1,1,128]+weight_v[768,48,128], k=128 groups=16, converter must fuse), encoder input-side LayerNorm[768], DomainEmbedding[3,128] (inference const domains=0), judge Embedding[3000,128] (inference const judge_id=288), bidirectional LSTM 1024→512×2 (8 tensors), and a ReLU between the two head linears (1024→2048→1); the ratified characterization 'wav2vec2 SSL + regression head' omits the listener/domain conditioning and BLSTM

P2: kernel gap — vokra-backend-cpu/vokra-ops have NO lstm and NO group_norm kernel, and Kokoro BiLstm1d lives in vokra-models (banned vokra-eval→vokra-models edge per ADR M4-18-utmos-arch (B')), so the flip must add an LSTM impl reachable from vokra-eval (extract into vokra-backend-cpu kernels or scalar-loop in eval; Kokoro parity precedent favors scalar loop)

P2 (license, positive): entire weight chain is permissive with live evidence — HF space MIT (cardData+LICENSE), UTMOS22 GitHub MIT, fairseq MIT / HF wav2vec2-base apache-2.0; BVCC (Zenodo 10691660, license 'other-open', open access) bans only redistribution of Blizzard SAMPLES; no academic-only clause found anywhere = M4-18 fail-closed trigger NOT tripped by evidence (owner T02 sign-off still required; record Blizzard/VCC-derived-weights caveat in license-audit Notes)

P3: DNSMOS 'research-only' fear not confirmed by primary sources — microsoft/DNS-Challenge Legal Notices grant CC-BY-4.0 for content (LICENSE) + MIT for code (LICENSE-CODE), P.835 ONNX models are committed in-repo (DNSMOS/DNSMOS/*.onnx + pDNSMOS/sig_bak_ovr.onnx); T03 likely resolvable as GO-with-attribution (Mimi CC-BY-4.0 NOTICE precedent)
P3: released ckpt contains NO PhonemeEncoder tensors (demo = 'UTMOS-strong w/o phoneme encoder') — no G2P path needed for the flip, a scope reduction vs the repo's full strong-learner training code

P3: parity-fixture env wrinkle — the pinned reference env (torch 1.11.0 + fairseq d03f4e77 + PL 1.5.10 per space requirements.txt) has no macOS-arm64 torch wheel, so the T09 honest-atol reproduction band on this M1 must be measured across an explicit env delta or on an x86/Linux runner; also the demo's own two inference paths disagree on resampling (score.py sinc vs app.py linear interp) → fixtures must use native 16 kHz clips
P3: conv1d_f32 kernel lacks grouped-conv support (pos_conv needs groups=16) — composable as 16 sliced conv1d calls without kernel change; padding=64 + even-kernel trailing-frame trim also expressible

P3: converter detail — strong ckpt already excludes the 7 pretraining tensors (quantizer/project_q/final_proj) vs wav2vec_small.pt, but retains unused mask_emb[768] which the converter should drop; total mapping surface = 224 tensors / 103.16M params
issues: Both checkpoints fail torch.load(weights_only=True) (Lightning 1.5 hyper_parameters carry omegaconf DictConfig; fairseq ckpt carries argparse/fairseq objects) — metadata recon used a tolerant unpickler stubbing unimportable classes; the flip-time converter must plan the same (or install omegaconf) and this is recorded in dump-metadata.log
hyper_parameters cfg (hydra model config) was only partially readable through stubs — fully readable at flip time with omegaconf installed; state_dict evidence is complete regardless

fairseq args lack an explicit layer_norm_first key in this 2020-07 checkpoint — post-norm placement is instead proven by tensor evidence (per-block self_attn_layer_norm/final_layer_norm + input-side encoder.layer_norm) and must be double-pinned against fairseq@d03f4e77 source at flip time (noted in memo)
No score/parity number was produced by design (investigation-only leg; producing one would override the ratified NO-GO-defer gate)

accuracy.methodology: No inference and no parity comparison were run (ratified defer gate respected). Measured facts: state_dict census via torch 2.8 CPU load — UTMOS22-strong = 225 tensors / 103,156,993 params (SSL 211 / 94,371,712 + head 14 / 8,785,281); wav2vec_small.pt = 218 tensors / 95,044,608 params; SSL key-set diff = exactly 7 pretraining-only tensors absent from the fine-tuned ckpt; conv spec [(512,10,5)]+[(512,3,2)]*4+[(512,2,2)]*2, encoder 12L/768d/12h/3072ffn read from fairseq args; inference constants domains=0/judge_id=288 and score formula mean(t)*2+3 read verbatim from space score.py/app.py; file sizes cross-checked HF tree API vs downloaded bytes vs fbaipublicfiles Content-Length (exact match), sha256 recorded
accuracy.skeleton_vs_real: 8 of 15 compared components inexpressible in ARCH_VARIANT_V0 (see skeleton-vs-real-delta.tsv); trunk dims, post-norm placement, pooling and affine (scale=2, offset=3) match v0 config surface

speed.note: Not applicable — investigation-only leg; no model inference executed, so no RTF/latency was measured. Only wall-clock observation: 2.19 GB checkpoint download from HF completed in ~37 s.

summary: First real-weight Moshi verification: downloaded kyutai/moshiko-pytorch-bf16 (14.32 GiB, sha256 5b835c66... = HF blob hash, live license check cc-by-4.0), and its 355-tensor header matches the committed T02 manifest fixture exactly. Full-7B conversion/dump/load are all honestly BLOCKED on 16 GB RAM (converter materializes ~3.4x output size = ~97 GiB measured-scaling; torch fp32 dump ~43 GiB; MoshiEngine::from_path ~60 GiB, no mmap wiring), so I built a truncated-but-real-weights checkpoint (temporal 32→2, depformer 6→2 layers, 95 tensors incl. all embeddings/heads/glue, bytes verbatim) and ran the REAL vokra-convert on it: 95/95 BF16→F32 exact (byte-verified on 257M elements), tokenizer embedded, hparams shape-derived correctly, median 19.56 s. The repo's env-gated parity harness (parity_moshi.rs) genuinely fired against a matching truncated torch fp32 reference (moshi 0.2.13, argmax LMGen, 12 steps): backbone hidden max |Δ| = 8.249e-5, text logits max |Δ| = 2.360e-5 (atol 0.01), and all 11 emitted frames bit-exact through the full greedy loop (temporal + depformer + delay ring) — PASS. Bounded duplex execution via vokra-cli (--duplex --echo-sim 0.3 --deterministic) processed 5/25/100 mic frames → equal model frames with finite audio and real-SentencePiece monologue decode; load ≈ 24.1 s, per-frame ≈ 0.699 s (RTF ≈ 8.7 vs the 80 ms frame budget, truncated model, M1 CPU), and both attribution surfaces (CLI banner + C ABI vokra_model_attribution two-call, 333 bytes) returned the exact CC-BY 4.0 Kyutai text from the real GGUF. CSM probe: metadata HTTP 200 (gated: auto, license apache-2.0) but file resolve HTTP 401 "Access to model sesame/csm-1b is restricted..." with the stale token — owner needs gate acceptance + fresh token.

findings: P2: the full-7B Moshi real-weight chain cannot execute on 16 GB-class RAM at ANY stage — (a) convert_moshi_file materializes input Vec (14.32 GiB) + F32 builder copies (28.64 GiB) + to_bytes output Vec (28.64 GiB); measured footprint scaling 3.4x output size (15.1 GiB at 4.14 GiB output) extrapolates to ~97 GiB; (b) moshi_dump.py real uses get_moshi_lm dtype=fp32 => ~43 GiB (14.32 bf16 state dict + 28.64 fp32 casts); (c) MoshiEngine::from_path = fs::read + GgufFile::parse(bytes.to_vec()) = 2x GGUF (~60 GiB full) with vokra-mmap NOT wired for moshi. Consequence: .github/workflows/parity-moshi-real.yml pins runs-on: ubuntu-latest (GitHub-hosted standard runners are 16 GB-RAM-class per GitHub docs) — the T25 real-checkpoint workflow as written cannot complete there; needs a large-RAM self-hosted runner or a streaming converter + mmap load path
P3: duplex demo output audio peaks at 1.412 > 1.0 full-scale (float32 WAV; synthesized Mimi decoder bridge) — PCM16 consumers would clip; no limiter/normalization in the demo output path

P3: CLI attribution banner prints the license slot as the class slug 'attribution-required' (vokra: ATTRIBUTION (attribution-required) ...) instead of the SPDX id 'CC-BY-4.0' the converter stamped into provenance; the text body does contain 'CC-BY 4.0' so the display obligation is met — cosmetic

P3 (coverage gap): Moshi real-weight GPU verification is unreachable from the CLI — vokra-cli run (duplex) has no backend selector and moshi_metal_parity.rs is synthesized-only, so this leg's real-weight coverage is CPU-only

P3 (test-suite footgun, by design but worth flagging): with VOKRA_MOSHI_PARITY_DIR set, running the full vokra-models suite fails loudly in moshi_quality_gate.rs (deliberate refusal pending real Mimi binding) — the workflow avoids it by running --test parity_moshi only; owners running 'cargo test -p vokra-models' with the env var set will hit it
